channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel threatens 'all hell will break loose' on Hamas in latest Gaza ceasefire crisis
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s defense minister on Wednesday vowed that “all hell will break loose” on Hamas if it fails to free hostages this weekend as planned, stepping up threats against the militant group as mediators worked to salvage their ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 10 hospitalized in fire at Rio de Janeiro's Carnival costume factory
item.description: A fire in a factory that produces costumes for the carnival in Rio de Janeiro has left 10 people hospitalized, health authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Diarlei Rodrigues And Eléonore Hughes, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Things to know about Russia's release of American Marc Fogel and the impact on ending Ukraine war
item.description: The U.S. and Russia agreed to a prisoner swap involving Marc Fogel, an American schoolteacher who the Biden administration had deemed wrongfully detained by Russia, in a diplomatic move that the White House said could move forward negotiations to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: White House says American among three freed in Belarus
item.description: The White House said Wednesday that President Trump secured the release of at least three people jailed in Belarus, including an American it has not named and a Belarusian journalist.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Protests after 2 sudden deaths force closure of hospital trauma unit in Central African Republic
item.description: BANGUI, Central African Republic (AP) — The sudden closure in the Central African Republic of the orthopedic trauma surgery unit at the capital 's main hospital following the deaths of two people, including a government minister's niece, has sparked concern among patients and relatives.
item.dc:creator: JEAN-FERNAND KOENA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria's interim foreign minister says Russia-Iran ties to Assad are an open wound after war
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Syria's relations with Iran and Russia remain an open wound for its people after those nations backed autocratic President Bashar Assad during the long civil war, the country's new, interim foreign minister said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Italy prepares scenic, historic Olympic venues with games now one year away
item.description: This marks the first time Milan will host the Winter Olympics, with the opening ceremony scheduled at San Siro Stadium. The games will feature events in multiple locations, including hockey in a new arena in Milan and various alpine events in Cortina.
item.dc:creator: Matt Barnes, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US releasing Russian prisoner Alexander Vinnik in Marc Fogel exchange, official says
item.description: The United States is releasing Russian prisoner Alexander Vinnik as part of the deal to secure Marc Fogel's freedom, a Trump administration official said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN halts humanitarian work in Yemen's Houthi stronghold after staff detentions
item.description: The United Nations has suspended humanitarian operations in northern Saada after rebels detained eight more U.N. staffers, during one of the world's worst humanitarian crises.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fogel release a sign of Trump, Putin's 'great friendship': Witkoff
item.description: Trump and Putin had a "great friendship, and I think now it's going to continue," special envoy Steve Witkoff said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Austrian far-right leader's efforts to form a new government collapse
item.description: Austrian far-right leader Herbert Kickl says his talks on forming a coalition government with a conservative party have collapsed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jailed Russian released in US in exchange for Marc Fogel: Kremlin
item.description: The Kremlin said early Wednesday that American Marc Fogel's release came in conjunction with the Trump administration granting freedom to a Russian citizen held in a U.S. prison — though they were not identified.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: UN rights office estimates up to 1,400 killed in crackdown on protests in Bangladesh
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The U.N. human rights office on Wednesday estimated that up to 1,400 people may have been killed in Bangladesh over three weeks last summer in a crackdown on student-led protests against the now-ousted former prime minister.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Freed American hostage Marc Fogel lands in US after years in Russian captivity
item.description: Marc Fogel, an American who had been detained in Russia since 2021, landed in the U.S. on Tuesday after his release from Russia following talks with the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis accepts the resignation of a Peruvian bishop accused of ignoring abuse
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Pope Francis on Tuesday accepted the resignation of an influential Peruvian bishop, who has come under criticism for not doing enough to stop abuses committed by a conservative Catholic movement that was recently disbanded by the Vatican.
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he may withhold aid to Jordan, Egypt if they don’t take in Palestinians
item.description: President Trump said Monday he would consider withholding aid to Jordan and Egypt if they do not agree to take in Palestinians as part of a plan for the U.S. to take over and develop the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Venezuelan planes sent to US for deportation flights return to country with nearly 200 deportees
item.description: Two planes sent by Venezuela returned to the country from El Paso, Texas, on Monday with nearly 200 Venezuelans who were in the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ecuador's presidential election goes to runoff between conservative incumbent, leftist lawyer
item.description: Ecuador will choose its next president in a runoff election in April between conservative incumbent Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González. Crime is a major issue for voters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas says it's delaying next hostage release, claiming ceasefire violations
item.description: Hamas is delaying the next release of Oct. 7 hostages, accusing Israel of violating the terms of their ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, South Africa in growing row over hotly contested land law, country's deals with US foes
item.description: Trump’s executive order cutting aid and offering refugee status to South Africa’s Afrikaner farmers has caused a stir in the African country after passage of a controversial land bill.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Days before Trump halted funding, an ex-Israeli hostage was held at UNRWA school in Gaza, she reveals
item.description: Former Israeli hostage Emily Damari said she was held in an UNRWA center in Gaza. Damari was recently released as part of the hostages for ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Defaced Holocaust mural finds new home in Rome's Shoah Museum
item.description: Contemporary pop artist aleXsandro Palombo is speaking out after his murals showing Holocaust survivors were defaced multiple times in Italy in apparent acts of antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One of the victims of Sweden's deadliest mass shooting called his fiancée to say he loved her
item.description: OREBRO, Sweden (AP) — Wounded and bleeding, Salim Karim Iskef managed to video-call his fiancée to tell her he loved her one last time before he died this week in what was Sweden's deadliest mass shooting, The 28-year-old asked Kareen Alia to look after his mother and herself before the call ended.
item.dc:creator: ALEKSANDAR FURTULA, SERGEI GRITS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: ICC condemns sanctions by Trump, pledges to continue its work
item.description: The International Criminal Court said the move was an attempt to “harm its independent and impartial judicial work.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Egypt lobbies against Trump plan to empty Gaza of Palestinians as Israel prepares for it
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel says it has begun preparations for the departure of Palestinians from Gaza despite international rejection of President Donald Trump’s plan to empty the territory of its population.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Zelenskyy to lead Ukraine’s delegation at Munich Security Conference which JD Vance will attend
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will lead Ukraine’s delegation at the Munich Security Conference next week, which U.S. Vice President J.D.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9
